lessons.text: [Round 4][Purchase Units][Mid][China Consolidation] Japan purchased 2 fighters (20 PU) and 1 artillery (4 PU), totaling 24 PU, but failed to address the transport bottleneck with only 2 transports available for 6 infantry on Japan. Should have purchased 1 transport (7 PU) and 2 infantry (6 PU) to enable effective troop deployment to mainland Asia.
lessons.rag_text:
[Round 4][Purchase Units][Mid][China Consolidation] Japan purchased 2 fighters (20 PU) and 1 artillery (4 PU), totaling 24 PU, but failed to address the transport bottleneck with only 2 transports available for 6 infantry on Japan. Should have purchased 1 transport (7 PU) and 2 infantry (6 PU) to enable effective troop deployment to mainland Asia.
[Principle] When japan_land ≥ 6 and transports ≤ 2, prioritize buying transports over additional land units to ensure effective deployment.
lessons.generalized_principle: When japan_land ≥ 6 and transports ≤ 2, prioritize buying transports over additional land units to ensure effective deployment.

structured.trigger: Round 4, Japan has 6 infantry on island, only 2 transports available, China has 4 infantry in Yunnan.
structured.mistake: Round 4 Purchase: bought 2 fighters instead of 1 transport and 2 infantry, leaving infantry stranded on Japan.
structured.action: In Purchase Units phase of round 4, buy 1 transport and 2 infantry to resolve transport bottleneck.
structured.expected_outcome: Enable transport of 4 infantry to mainland, increasing pressure on Chinese territories.
structured.legal_action_required: buy 1 transport, buy 2 infantry

lessons.text: [Round 5][Combat Move][Mid][China Consolidation] Japan attempted to capture Kwangtung but failed due to insufficient forces. Attacked with 2 infantry and 1 artillery from Kwangsi against 3 UK infantry, resulting in a failed attack. Should have committed 3 infantry and 1 artillery to ensure a successful capture.
lessons.rag_text:
[Round 5][Combat Move][Mid][China Consolidation] Japan attempted to capture Kwangtung but failed due to insufficient forces. Attacked with 2 infantry and 1 artillery from Kwangsi against 3 UK infantry, resulting in a failed attack. Should have committed 3 infantry and 1 artillery to ensure a successful capture.
[Principle] Always commit ≥ 1.5x defender count when attacking; if ratio < 1.5, add aircraft support or defer to next round.
lessons.generalized_principle: Always commit ≥ 1.5x defender count when attacking; if ratio < 1.5, add aircraft support or defer to next round.

structured.trigger: Round 5, Kwangtung has 3 UK infantry defenders, Japan has 2 infantry + 1 artillery in Kwangsi available.
structured.mistake: Round 5 Combat Move: attacked Kwangtung with insufficient forces, resulting in failure.
structured.action: In Combat Move phase of round 5, attack Kwangtung with 3 infantry and 1 artillery from Kwangsi.
structured.expected_outcome: Capture Kwangtung (IPC +3), open route to French Indo-China next round.
structured.legal_action_required: attack Kwangtung with 3 infantry 1 artillery from Kwangsi

lessons.text: [Round 5][Noncombat Move][Mid][China Consolidation] Japan moved 2 infantry from Manchuria to Kiangsu to prepare for future operations against Yunnan and Kwangtung. This move was necessary to bolster forces in the region and maintain pressure on Chinese territories.
lessons.rag_text:
[Round 5][Noncombat Move][Mid][China Consolidation] Japan moved 2 infantry from Manchuria to Kiangsu to prepare for future operations against Yunnan and Kwangtung. This move was necessary to bolster forces in the region and maintain pressure on Chinese territories.
[Principle] In NCM, always reinforce staging areas with sufficient infantry to maintain offensive momentum in adjacent territories.
lessons.generalized_principle: In NCM, always reinforce staging areas with sufficient infantry to maintain offensive momentum in adjacent territories.

structured.legal_action_required: move 2 infantry from Manchuria to Kiangsu

lessons.text: [Round 2][Purchase Units][Early][China Consolidation] Japan purchased 1 battleship (20 PU) and 1 infantry (3 PU), totaling 23 PU, which was an inefficient allocation of resources given the need for ground forces in China. Should have purchased 3 infantry (9 PU) and 1 artillery (4 PU) to strengthen land operations.
lessons.rag_text:
[Round 2][Purchase Units][Early][China Consolidation] Japan purchased 1 battleship (20 PU) and 1 infantry (3 PU), totaling 23 PU, which was an inefficient allocation of resources given the need for ground forces in China. Should have purchased 3 infantry (9 PU) and 1 artillery (4 PU) to strengthen land operations.
[Principle] Prioritize ground forces over naval units when immediate land operations are critical to strategic goals.
lessons.generalized_principle: Prioritize ground forces over naval units when immediate land operations are critical to strategic goals.

structured.trigger: Round 2, Japan has 3 infantry in Kiangsu, China has 5 infantry in Yunnan, naval superiority not contested.
structured.mistake: Round 2 Purchase: bought 1 battleship instead of 3 infantry and 1 artillery, weakening ground operations.
structured.action: In Purchase Units phase of round 2, buy 3 infantry and 1 artillery to support land operations in China.
structured.expected_outcome: Increase ground force strength in Kiangsu, enabling stronger attacks on Yunnan.
structured.legal_action_required: buy 3 infantry, buy 1 artillery

lessons.text: [Round 3][Combat Move][Early][China Consolidation] Japan successfully captured Kiangsi with overwhelming force, using 4 infantry and 1 artillery from Kwangsi, supported by 5 tactical bombers and 5 fighters from Kiangsu. This ensured a decisive victory and opened a path southward, with a total of 10 attacking units against 2 defenders.
lessons.rag_text:
[Round 3][Combat Move][Early][China Consolidation] Japan successfully captured Kiangsi with overwhelming force, using 4 infantry and 1 artillery from Kwangsi, supported by 5 tactical bombers and 5 fighters from Kiangsu. This ensured a decisive victory and opened a path southward, with a total of 10 attacking units against 2 defenders.
[Principle] Use overwhelming force to ensure victory in strategically important territories, aiming for a force ratio of at least 2:1.
lessons.generalized_principle: Use overwhelming force to ensure victory in strategically important territories, aiming for a force ratio of at least 2:1.

structured.trigger: Round 3, Kiangsi has 2 Chinese infantry defenders, Japan has 4 infantry + 1 artillery in Kwangsi, air support available from Kiangsu.
structured.mistake: Round 3 Combat Move: none, executed with overwhelming force ensuring success, but should have noted the specific unit counts for clarity.
structured.action: In Combat Move phase of round 3, attack Kiangsi with 4 infantry, 1 artillery, and air support from Kwangsi and Kiangsu.
structured.expected_outcome: Capture Kiangsi, compress Chinese forces, and open path to southern territories.
structured.legal_action_required: attack Kiangsi with 4 infantry 1 artillery from Kwangsi, air support from Kiangsu

lessons.text: [Round 3][Noncombat Move][Early][China Consolidation] Japan failed to utilize transports effectively, leaving 2 transports idle in 6 Sea Zone while 4 infantry remained on Japan. Should have transported 2 infantry to mainland to support operations in China.
lessons.rag_text:
[Round 3][Noncombat Move][Early][China Consolidation] Japan failed to utilize transports effectively, leaving 2 transports idle in 6 Sea Zone while 4 infantry remained on Japan. Should have transported 2 infantry to mainland to support operations in China.
[Principle] Dispatch all empty transports every NCM — an idle transport is 7 PU wasted per round.
lessons.generalized_principle: Dispatch all empty transports every NCM — an idle transport is 7 PU wasted per round.

structured.trigger: Round 3, Japan has 4 infantry on island, 2 transports idle in 6 Sea Zone, China has 3 infantry in Yunnan.
structured.mistake: Round 3 Noncombat Move: left 2 transports idle, failing to reinforce mainland operations.
structured.action: In Noncombat Move phase of round 3, transport 2 infantry from Japan to mainland via 6 Sea Zone.
structured.expected_outcome: Increase ground forces on mainland, enhancing operational capability in China.
structured.legal_action_required: transport 2 infantry from Japan via 6 Sea Zone to mainland
